title: chamaecytisus triflorus subsp. pumilus
intro: Chamaecytisus triflorus subsp. pumilus, also known as the dwarf broom or dwarf Spanish broom, is a low-growing, spiny shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high concentration of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Chamaecytisus triflorus subsp. pumilus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
